comment: This is generated by ESQL's AbstractFunctionTestCase. Do not edit it. See ../README.md for how to regenerate it.
name: json_extract
description:
Extracts a value from a JSON string using a subset of
JSONPath syntax.

params.name: string
params.description: A string containing valid JSON, or the `_source` field. If `null`, the function returns `null`.

params.name: path
params.description: A path expression identifying the value to extract, using a subset of JSONPath syntax. Supports dot notation (`user.name`), bracket notation for array indices (`items[0]`), and quoted brackets for keys with special characters (`['user.name']`). The `$` prefix is optional. If `null`, the function returns `null`.

If `null`, the function returns `null`.

examples:
ROW json = "[\\"a\\",\\"b\\",\\"c\\"]"
| EVAL val = JSON_EXTRACT(json, "$[1]")
| KEEP val

ROW json = "{\\"company\\":{\\"departments\\":[{\\"name\\":\\"eng\\",\\"leads\\":[{\\"name\\":\\"Alice\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Bob\\"}]},{\\"name\\":\\"sales\\",\\"leads\\":[{\\"name\\":\\"Carol\\"}]}]}}"
| EVAL lead = JSON_EXTRACT(json, "company.departments[0].leads[1].name")
| KEEP lead
